place: Forest Home CDP, New York, New York

facts.text: Forest Home’s civic heart began in 1920 when the Forest Home Improvement Association was founded to serve the growing residential community.
Two historic single‑lane riveted steel‑truss bridges span Fall Creek, linking Forest Home Drive to the rest of the hamlet.
The neighborhood evolved from a 19th‑century water‑powered mill center into the quiet residential enclave it is today.
Forest Home sits on gently rolling hills, with an average elevation of about 387 feet above sea level.
The IBM corporate headquarters, a landmark of modern technology, is located in nearby Armonk, just a short drive from Forest Home.
Howard Stern, the nationally known radio host, called Forest Home home from 1990 to 2010.
